text:
There once was a dog who loved pretzels.

One chilly autumn afternoon, our little pup found himself nose près of the attic window. That's where Grandma kept her ancient pretzel crumbs.

With great determination, he leapt onto the old oak shelf. His jaws worked with such focus. Soon enough, he dove into that treasure trove.

Every bite was perfection. He'd snuggle under Dad's coat afterward.

One day, Dad saw him munching like that. "You should try some cheese!" he said.

That turned strange, but OK. They found a soft cheese ball. Dad tossed it.

Our buddy couldn't snatch it quick enough. His tongue chased.

He paused. Cheese!

That melted.

He grabbed loads.

Now had fun with everything.

We'd go around town. There was nothing special.

But one day.

There was a little girl begging.

"Please have mozzarella."

She loved cheese ice cream.

So Dad bought it.

Our dog's eyes lit up
